channel.title: Internode Content
channel.description: Root-level answers, use cases, and updates from Internode.

item.title: A business case template for a knowledge management tool (that your manager will actually read)
item.description: A copy-paste business case template for a knowledge management tool, structured the way managers actually read proposals: problem, cost of inaction, options, ROI, rollout plan, and success metrics.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: AI knowledge management for consultants: stop losing what you learn across client engagements
item.description: A practical look at AI knowledge management for consultants: how to capture, connect, and retrieve what you learn across many client engagements without building a database by hand.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: AI knowledge management for government: preserve institutional knowledge through elections, turnover, and FOIA
item.description: How public agencies use AI knowledge management to preserve institutional memory across elected official turnover, FOIA requests, and multi-year programs, with records retention handled honestly.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: AI meeting notes for schools: capture board decisions, staff meetings, and IEP conversations without anyone taking minutes
item.description: How schools use AI meeting notes to capture decisions from board meetings, staff sessions, and IEP conversations into a searchable record, with FERPA and compliance handled honestly.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: AI meeting prep for executive assistants: draft the brief your executive will actually read
item.description: How executive assistants use AI meeting prep to draft briefs their executives will actually read, grounded in every past conversation, email, and commitment with each stakeholder.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: AI phone call transcription for small business: turn every supplier, customer, and staff call into usable knowledge
item.description: How small businesses use AI phone call transcription to capture pricing, orders, commitments, and follow-ups from calls without typing notes or keeping a CRM.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: AI PM agent: what it actually is and what to demand from one
item.description: An AI PM agent captures, organizes, and changes project state from conversations and chat. Here is what separates a real one from a meeting-notes tool with a chat box.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: An AI PM that captures tasks from meetings
item.description: An AI PM that captures tasks from meetings should produce real tasks linked to the moment in the meeting they came from, not bullet lists inside a transcript. Here is what that looks like in practice.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: How to calculate the ROI of an AI knowledge tool (for the manager you're trying to convince)
item.description: A step-by-step ROI calculator for an AI knowledge tool, with worked examples for a 20-person team, so you can show your manager a real number instead of a vague productivity claim.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How to propose new software to your manager (without getting shut down)
item.description: A practical playbook for proposing new software internally: how to frame the problem, pre-empt procurement and security objections, use a pilot, quantify the pain, and name the tool at the right moment.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How to stop typing tasks from meetings
item.description: A practical guide to ending the habit of retyping meeting action items into Linear, Jira, or Asana after every call. Four changes, one outcome: the plan stays current without a human scribe.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: How to synthesize knowledge across client meetings (without relying on memory)
item.description: A step-by-step guide to synthesizing knowledge across many client meetings using structured capture, records for decisions and tasks, and cross-meeting topic clustering instead of memory or manual notes.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: Internode vs Asana AI Studio for work plans: which AI plan reflects what your team actually decided?
item.description: Internode vs Asana AI Studio compared on the axes that matter for AI work plans: building a plan from decisions made in meetings, cross-source grounding across meetings, email, and chat, and two-way sync with Linear or Jira.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Asana AI: which AI task manager should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Asana AI compared on the axes that matter for an AI PM agent: capture from conversations, decision-to-task provenance, bulk mutations, and cross-meeting deduplication.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs ChatGPT for documents: which AI drafts from your team's memory, not just the prompt?
item.description: Internode vs ChatGPT for documents compared on the axes that matter: drafting from the team's decision history, section-level citations, a research loop across your own memory, and auto-updating when decisions change.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs ClickUp AI for work plans: which AI builds a plan from your team's decisions?
item.description: Internode vs ClickUp AI compared on the axes that matter for AI work plans: building a work plan from decisions made in meetings, tracing plan sections back to source conversations, and syncing the generated tasks two-way with Linear or Jira.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Coda AI for living documents: which AI updates the doc from the real world?
item.description: Internode vs Coda AI compared on the axes that matter for living documents: auto-updating from meetings, email, and chat, tracing every update to a source event, and drafting through a proposal flow rather than silent edits.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Confluence AI: which AI knowledge base should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Confluence AI compared on the axes that matter for an AI knowledge base: conversations as input, structured records, the decision-to-source trail, and memory-aware drafting.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Fathom: which AI drafts the meeting brief before you walk in?
item.description: Internode vs Fathom compared on the drafting axis: grounding in the team's decision history, cross-meeting context, per-section citations, and the research loop that composes a pre-meeting brief.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Fellow: which AI drafts from your team's decision history?
item.description: Internode vs Fellow compared on the axes that matter for AI document drafting: team-wide decision history, section-level citations, and auto-updating when decisions change.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Fireflies AI: which AI drafts the meeting brief from your team's memory?
item.description: Internode vs Fireflies AI compared on the drafting axis: grounding in the team's decision history, cross-meeting context, per-section citations, and the research loop behind a real pre-meeting brief.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Gemini for documents: which AI drafts grounded in your team's memory?
item.description: Internode vs Gemini for documents compared on the axes that matter: drafting from the team's decision history, section-level citations, and auto-updating when decisions change.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Glean: which AI drafts from your organization's real decisions?
item.description: Internode vs Glean compared on the axes that matter for AI document drafting: the team's decision history, section-level citations, a research loop across your own memory, and auto-updating when decisions change.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Granola Prep: which AI drafts the meeting brief you'll actually read?
item.description: Internode vs Granola Prep compared on the drafting axis: grounding in the team's decision history, cross-meeting context, per-section citations, and a research loop behind the pre-meeting brief.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Granola: which AI meeting intelligence tool should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Granola compared on the axes that matter for AI meeting intelligence: phone call capture, email threads, decision rationale, bulk changes from chat, two-way Linear and Jira sync, and organizational search.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Guru: which AI knowledge base should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Guru compared on the axes that matter for an AI knowledge base: conversations as input, structured records, the decision-to-source trail, and organizational memory.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Jira: which AI PM agent should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Jira compared on the axes that matter for an AI PM agent: capture from conversations, decision-to-task provenance, compound proposals, and two-way sync back into Jira.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Letta: which memory layer should your AI agent use?
item.description: Internode vs Letta compared on the axes that matter for AI agent memory: team-scoped structured memory, a decision-to-source trail, ingestion from real conversations, and two-way sync to operational tools.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Linear: which AI PM agent should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Linear compared on the axes that matter for an AI PM agent: capture from conversations, decision-to-task provenance, bulk mutations, and two-way sync.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Mem0: which memory layer should your AI agent use?
item.description: Internode vs Mem0 compared on the axes that matter for AI agent memory: team-scoped structured memory, a decision-to-source trail, ingestion from real conversations, and two-way sync to operational tools.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Microsoft Copilot for documents: which AI drafts from your team's actual decisions?
item.description: Internode vs Microsoft Copilot for documents compared on the axes that matter: drafting from the team's decision history, section-level citations to source conversations, and a research loop across the team's own memory before writing.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Microsoft Syntex for policy-grounded documents: which AI grounds drafts in both your policies AND your team's decisions?
item.description: Internode vs Microsoft Syntex compared on the axes that matter for policy-grounded documents: grounding drafts in both company policy and the team's live decisions, updating when either source changes, and citing policy sections alongside the source decision per paragraph.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Notion AI for documents: which AI drafts from your team's memory?
item.description: Internode vs Notion AI for documents compared on the axes that matter: drafting from the team's decision history, section-level citations, and auto-updating when decisions change.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Notion AI: which AI should manage your team's knowledge?
item.description: Internode vs Notion AI compared on the axes that matter for AI knowledge management: conversations as input, structured records, decision-to-source trail, cross-meeting matching, memory-aware drafting, and organizational search with sources attached.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Notion as a wiki: which AI knowledge base should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Notion as a wiki compared on the axes that matter for an AI knowledge base: conversations as input, structured records, the decision-to-source trail, and zero page-writing.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Otter: which AI drafts a meeting brief from your team's knowledge?
item.description: Internode vs Otter compared on the drafting axis: grounding in the team's decision history, cross-meeting context, per-section citations, and the research loop that composes a real pre-meeting brief.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Otter: which AI meeting intelligence tool should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Otter compared on the axes that matter for AI meeting intelligence: phone call capture, email threads, structured tasks, decision rationale, two-way Linear and Jira sync, and organizational search.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Read AI: which AI meeting intelligence tool should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Read AI compared on the axes that matter for AI meeting intelligence: phone call capture, email threads, structured tasks, decision rationale, two-way Linear and Jira sync, and organizational search.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Slab: which AI knowledge base should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Slab compared on the axes that matter for an AI knowledge base: conversations as input, structured records, the decision-to-source trail, and memory-aware drafting.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs tldv: which AI drafts the meeting brief your team will actually use?
item.description: Internode vs tldv compared on the drafting axis: grounding in the team's decision history, cross-meeting context, per-section citations, and the research loop behind a pre-meeting brief teams actually read.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Zep: which memory layer should your AI agent use?
item.description: Internode vs Zep compared on the axes that matter for AI agent memory: team-scoped structured memory, a decision-to-source trail, ingestion from real conversations, and two-way sync to operational tools.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Memory-aware drafting: documents that know what your team already decided
item.description: Memory-aware drafting is document generation grounded in your team's organizational memory: every paragraph traces back to a decision, a meeting, or a policy your team already produced.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: The AI knowledge base that builds itself
item.description: Most AI knowledge bases are wikis with a chat box. A knowledge base that builds itself is one your team never has to update because it learns from the conversations that already happen.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: The AI-native alternative to Notion: a knowledge system that writes itself
item.description: Notion is a workspace-as-database that requires you to build the system. An AI-native alternative starts from conversations and produces structured knowledge without you typing pages.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: The alternative to a CRM for consulting knowledge: organize what you learn, not just who you spoke to
item.description: CRMs track contacts and deals. Consultants need a system that tracks what they learned from conversations, the decisions clients are weighing, and patterns across engagements. This is the alternative.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: The best AI knowledge management tools in 2026
item.description: A ranked look at the AI knowledge management tools that matter in 2026, ordered by how much of the work the tool still asks you to do yourself.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: The best AI task manager in 2026
item.description: A ranked comparison of the best AI task managers in 2026 on the axes that decide whether your plan stays current: capture from conversations, decision-to-task provenance, bulk mutations, and two-way sync.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: The best second brain app 2026: an honest ranking for people who have tried them all
item.description: An honest 2026 ranking of second brain apps for people who have already tried Notion, Obsidian, Roam, and Logseq. Internode ranks first because it is the only one that builds itself.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: The cost of lost team knowledge, per employee, per year
item.description: A per-employee dollar figure for lost team knowledge, built from research by IDC, McKinsey, Panopto, and Gartner on time lost searching, rework, duplicated decisions, and onboarding drag.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: What is decision memory?
item.description: Decision memory is the slice of your team's knowledge that holds final decisions, their rationale, and how they have changed over time. Here is what it is and where it fits inside organizational memory.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: What is organizational memory?
item.description: Organizational memory is the structured, searchable record of what your team has decided, agreed, and committed to across meetings, calls, and chat. Here is what it contains and why it matters.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: AI meeting notes versus organizational memory
item.description: A clear comparison of AI meeting note tools and organizational memory systems, explaining what each does well and where the gap lies for engineering and product teams.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: AI tools for knowledge management in government and public organizations
item.description: A guide to AI tools that help government agencies, schools, healthcare organizations, and nonprofits manage institutional knowledge and track outcomes.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: AI-first vs AI-added: why bolting AI onto Notion is not enough
item.description: AI-added tools bolt intelligence onto manual workflows. AI-first tools eliminate the manual layer entirely. The difference determines whether your knowledge system survives or decays.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: Building a business case for organizational intelligence
item.description: A framework for building a business case for knowledge management tools, with ROI calculations, audience-specific framing, and answers to common objections.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: From conversations to knowledge: what professionals actually need
item.description: Professionals who work across meetings, client calls, and research need a system that turns conversational knowledge into searchable, connected intelligence without manual organization.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How executive assistants stop being the only person who remembers what was decided
item.description: Executive assistants carry the organizational memory for their executives. Here is how to move that knowledge out of your head and into a system that survives your next vacation.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: How healthcare teams keep care coordination decisions organized
item.description: How healthcare organizations track care coordination decisions across shifts, staff changes, and department meetings to maintain continuity and reduce repeated work.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How schools preserve institutional knowledge when staff leave
item.description: Practical strategies for schools and districts to preserve institutional knowledge when experienced staff transfer or retire.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: How small businesses stop losing information from phone calls
item.description: Practical steps for small businesses to stop losing customer details, pricing agreements, and commitments from phone conversations.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How solving your team's knowledge problem advances your career
item.description: Being the person who identifies and fixes a systemic knowledge problem at work is a career-defining contribution. Here is why it matters and how to make it visible.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How to build a briefing system that does not depend on your memory
item.description: Most EA briefing systems rely on personal memory and manual research. Here is how to build one that generates context automatically from past meetings and conversations.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: How to capture decisions from meetings without writing everything down
item.description: A practical guide to capturing what was decided, assigned, and discussed in meetings using transcription instead of manual note-taking.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: How to connect meeting decisions to project tasks
item.description: How to close the gap between what your team agrees to in meetings and what actually shows up in Linear or Jira, with traceable links between decisions and work.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: How to organize customer and supplier commitments without a CRM
item.description: How small businesses can track customer orders, supplier agreements, and verbal commitments without buying or learning a CRM.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How to propose a knowledge tool when you have no budget authority
item.description: A step-by-step guide for employees who want to propose a knowledge management tool to their manager when they do not control the budget.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How to tell if your team has a knowledge management problem
item.description: Seven warning signs that your team is losing knowledge from meetings, conversations, and staff transitions, and what to do when you spot the pattern.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How to track decisions from board meetings and committee sessions
item.description: A practical approach to tracking decisions from board meetings and committee sessions so they stay findable and usable long after the meeting ends.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: How to turn phone calls into searchable business knowledge
item.description: A practical guide to transcribing business phone calls and turning them into organized, searchable records your whole team can access.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: Knowledge management for people who gave up on knowledge management
item.description: If you have tried and abandoned Notion, Obsidian, Roam, or any other knowledge system, the problem was not you. Here is what a zero-maintenance alternative looks like.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: The hidden cost of scattered knowledge at work
item.description: Scattered knowledge costs teams thousands of hours per year in repeated work, slow onboarding, and forgotten follow-ups. Here is how to quantify the problem.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: The knowledge system that builds itself
item.description: A knowledge system that organizes itself from your conversations, meetings, and documents. No filing, no tagging, no maintenance.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: What changes when your team actually remembers what was decided
item.description: When what your team discussed and agreed on is preserved and searchable, meetings get shorter, onboarding gets faster, and follow-through improves. Here is what the shift looks like.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: What happens to your office when the EA leaves
item.description: When an executive assistant leaves, the organizational knowledge they carry disappears with them. Here is what that costs and how to prevent it.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: What is institutional knowledge and why teams lose it
item.description: A clear definition of institutional knowledge, why organizations lose it when staff leave, and what to do about it.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: What is organizational memory for AI agents?
item.description: A practical definition of organizational memory for AI agents, explaining what it contains, how it differs from raw search, and why it changes retrieval quality.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: What to look for in an AI knowledge management tool
item.description: A practical checklist for evaluating AI knowledge management tools, written for engineering leads and PMs who need to separate real capabilities from demo-ware.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Why AI agents need decision memory
item.description: Why retrieval alone is not enough for AI agents and how decision memory improves answer quality, speed, and trust.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Why note-taking apps fail knowledge workers
item.description: Note-taking apps assume your knowledge comes from reading and typing. For professionals whose knowledge comes from conversations, meetings, and synthesis across sources, the model is wrong.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Why small businesses forget what was decided and how to fix it
item.description: Why small businesses lose track of their own agreements and practical steps to fix the problem without adding complexity.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: Why your best work knowledge comes from conversations, not documents
item.description: The most valuable professional knowledge originates in conversations, not documents. Documents capture conclusions. Conversations capture the reasoning, context, and relationships that matter when you face a similar situation later.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: Why your meeting prep takes hours and how to cut it in half
item.description: Executive assistants spend 10 to 12 hours a week on meeting prep and follow-ups. Here is why it takes so long and how to reduce it dramatically.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Why your second brain keeps failing
item.description: Second brain systems fail because they turn you into a librarian of your own knowledge. The problem is the paradigm, not the tool.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Why your team keeps re-discussing the same decisions
item.description: Teams re-discuss the same decisions because what was agreed, and why, is not recorded anywhere findable. Here is why it happens and what to do about it.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: Executive assistant tracking decisions and follow-ups across 50 meetings a week
item.description: How an EA supporting three executives uses a persistent memory layer to track decisions, commitments, and follow-ups across 50+ meetings per week.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Healthcare team tracking decisions across shifts and staff changes
item.description: How a healthcare organization tracks care coordination decisions across shifts, department meetings, and staff transitions.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: New executive assistant onboarding without predecessor documentation
item.description: How a new EA uses a persistent knowledge base to onboard in weeks instead of months when no predecessor documentation exists.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: School district preserving knowledge across staff transitions
item.description: How a school district preserves institutional knowledge when principals transfer, teachers retire, and coordinators change roles.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: Small business capturing decisions from phone calls automatically
item.description: How a small reselling business captures customer and supplier details from phone calls using transcription and AI, without manual data entry.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: Turning calls and meetings into structured knowledge for any team
item.description: How teams of any size turn phone calls, Zoom meetings, and Slack conversations into structured, searchable knowledge they can reuse.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Use case: product and engineering alignment
item.description: How persistent knowledge tracking keeps product and engineering teams aligned without repeating debates about scope, priorities, and tradeoffs.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: How Internode works with phone transcripts, meeting recordings, and notes
item.description: A transparent explanation of how Internode processes phone transcripts, meeting recordings from Zoom and Google Meet, Slack conversations, and typed notes into structured knowledge.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode content hub launch
item.description: Why Internode now publishes root-level answer pages on content.internode.ai, designed for both human readers and AI systems.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode integrations with Zoom, Google Meet, Slack, and email
item.description: How Internode connects to Zoom, Google Meet, Slack, email, phone transcripts, and task trackers like Linear and Jira.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi
